Container.Name: Ollama
Container.Overview: Ollama allows you to run open-source large language models (like Llama 3, Mistral, and more) locally. This template is configured with Nvidia GPU passthrough support for single or dual/multiple GPUs. Requires the NVIDIA Driver Plugin installed on your Unraid host. [Note: This is a third-party community template and is not an official XML file from the application developers.]

Config.Name: Ollama API Port
Config.Description: Port that the Ollama API server listens on.

Config.Name: Ollama Models Path
Config.Description: Path to store downloaded models and configurations.

Config.Name: Nvidia Visible Devices
Config.Description: The GPU UUIDs or 'all' to expose to Ollama. For dual GPUs, keeping 'all' lets Ollama leverage both automatically. Otherwise, specify a comma-separated list of GPU UUIDs.

Config.Name: Nvidia Driver Capabilities
Config.Description: GPU driver capabilities exposed to the container (keep 'all' or 'compute,utility').

Config.Name: Ollama Keep Alive
Config.Description: How long a model stays loaded in VRAM after a request (e.g. 5m, 24h). Set to -1 to keep loaded indefinitely.

Config.Name: Ollama Num Parallel
Config.Description: Max number of parallel requests a single model instance will process. Increasing this requires more VRAM.

Config.Name: Ollama Max Loaded Models
Config.Description: Maximum number of different models loaded in VRAM simultaneously.

Config.Name: Ollama Context Length
Config.Description: Sets the default context window (memory size) for all models if not explicitly set by the model file.
